title: The California Retail Theft Reduction Act, a cornerstone of a pivotal bipartisan legislative package aimed at curbing retail crime, has cleared both houses of the Legislature and n

raw_text:
```json
{
  "claims": [
    "The California Retail Theft Reduction Act creates a new crime targeting 'serial' retail thieves, with a penalty of up to three years for possession of stolen property with intent to resell.",
    "Evidence of intent in the new law can include repeated conduct or possession of a quantity of goods inconsistent with personal use.",
    "The bill allows for the value of thefts from different victims to be aggregated to meet the threshold for grand theft.",
    "The relevant intent standard in the legislation can be met by evidence that acts involve the same defendant(s), are substantially similar in nature, or occur within a 90-day period.",
    "The legislation expands the use of diversion and rehabilitative programs for shoplifting and petty theft, increasing supervision and offering an opportunity for early discharge from probation upon program completion.",
    "The bill allows police to arrest for shoplifting based on a witness's sworn statement or video footage of the crime.",
    "The legislation extends the ability of police to keep repeat offenders and those committing organized retail crime in custody.",
    "The bill provides legal protection for businesses and retailers that report retail crime, preventing nuisance actions against them for doing so."
  ]
}
```
